titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the David Rakoff Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff, Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 10589081.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 27, 2024 at 23:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, datebooks, notebooks, financial papers, photographs, artwork, rubber stamps, printed materials, audiovisual materials, and other papers by, to, or relating to David Rakoff. Writings include multiple drafts of Rakoff's essay collections Fraud , Don't Get Too Comfortable , and Half Empty .

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from David Rakoff on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2012; and gift of Gina Rakoff, 2019, 2020.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 16 (film reels): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 17 (computer disks): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The David Rakoff Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David Rakoff Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: David Rakoff (1964-2012)
bioghist.p: David Rakoff was a Canadian writer based in New York City known for humorous non-fiction essays and as a contributor to the radio program This American Life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, datebooks, notebooks, financial papers, photographs, artwork, rubber stamps, printed materials, audiovisual materials, and other papers by, to, or relating to David Rakoff. Writings include multiple drafts of Rakoff's essay collections Fraud , Don't Get Too Comfortable , and Half Empty .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight series: I. Correspondence, 1949-2012. II. Writings, 1990s-2000s. III. Personal Papers, 1980s-2010s. IV. Printed Materials, 1990s-2008. V. Audiovisual Materials, 1980s. VI. Electronic Media, 2000s. VII. September 2019 Acquisition, 1976–2019. VIII. December 2020 Acquisition, 1968-2020.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically. Materials previously intermixed with correspondence are grouped together following the correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material was tightly folded into a long U-shape.

did.unittitle: Writings by David Rakoff and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings include typescripts, corrected proofs, and galleys for works including Fraud , Don't Get Too Comfortable , and Half Empty . See also Series I. Correspondence.

did.unittitle: Articles and reviews - typescripts, some with edits
"David Rakoff - New Script" - 3 clean typescript copies, removed from binder, with blank application to Actors' Equity Association
Don't Get Too Comfortable - second draft, corrected, with comments by Kendra Harpoter (Doubleday)
Don't Get Too Comfortable - typescripts with notes, commentary and letter by Bill Thomas (Doubleday)
eine DATA base - NAM JUNE PAIK - edition cantz
Fraud: Essays - commentary and corrections from Pamela Murray
Fraud: Essays - miscellaneous TS pages with corrections
Fraud: Essays - typescript, first draft with edits and artwork
Half Empty - typescript with edits and commentary by Bill Thomas (Doubleday)
Little Freida Mysteries - typescript with highlighting, and rehearsal schedule
The Little Freida Mysteries - typescript with notes and highlighting
"March Drama Department Play" - rough draft and untitled script, removed from binder with clipping
Miscellaneous script pages - typescript with edits and highlighting
Pages from a script with highlighting and edits, removed from binder

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal papers include diaries and day planners, financial papers, photographs and slides, artwork, and rubber stamps. See also Series I. Correspondence.

did.unittitle: Blank notebook with journal entries, sketches, miscellaneous inserts
Personal notebook - "Tokyo, Iceland, Aspen, Loch Ness..."
Don't Get Too Comfortable - publication agreement
Fraud - Financial papers and related correspondence
"Backstage Press" tag and receipts - [Removed from photographs]
Correspondence and ephemera related to photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally tied around the rubber stamps.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed materials include various New York periodicals and clippings of theatre reviews. See also Series I. Correspondence.

did.unittitle: The New York Times and Observatory
The New York Times - two copies
The New York Times Book Review- Paperback Best Sellers - Framed

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Camera Original "Pain D'Amour", David Rakoff / Tosh Ozawa

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: C.1 Single Lat., Striped P. D'A.
did.materialspec: There is no audio on this reel.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Audiovisual material. Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes CDs, DVDs and an external hard drive.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.physdesc: 1.59 Gigabytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.

did.unittitle: THE NATURALIZED Preview Screener FLICKER FLACKER FILMS
did.physdesc: 3.60 Gigabytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.
58.03 Megabytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.

did.unittitle: DAVID RAKOFF PHOTO SCANS & VOICEOVER VERSIONS
did.physdesc: 377.56 Megabytes. This reflects the size of the carrier media, but may not accurately reflect the extent of digital files available for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series primarily consists of condolence letters and cards to David Rakoff's family following his death in 2012 and printed material by or about Rakoff, in addition to early correspondence and personal papers of Rakoff.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Correspondence and Other Papers, and Printed Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains condolence letters to David Rakoff's family, ephemera from tributes to Rakoff, and materials related to posthumously published works, such as Love, Dishonor, Marry, Die, Cherish, Perish . Also included are school papers, financial documents, and letters from Rakoff to his family during his travels and studies in Israel and London in the 1980s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a few photographs of Rakoff.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photograph of Rakoff and Alexa Junge.

did.unittitle: For: Gina and Vivian Rakoff [materials related to David's writings and death]

title.part: Love, Dishonor, Marry, Die, Cherish, Perish

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of magazines, clippings, and computer printouts featuring writings by or about David Rakoff. Included are posthumous book reviews and articles, as well as a few clippings related to Rakoff's theater performances.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title, with clippings and printouts filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains writings, family and personal correspondence, artwork, ephemera, printed material, and a small amount of papers related to David Rakoff's high school and college years, including a pin from Columbia University. Also present are a Queen Elizabeth II Diamond Jubilee Medal, financial records, and a eulogy for Rakoff.

did.unittitle: Columbia University program and Dean's List pin
Correspondence [including illustrated envelopes and original artwork by Rakoff]
Love, Dishonor, Marry, Die, Cherish, Perish , draft, corrected
Woody Allen [printout of blog posts by Rakoff on Woody Allen Film Festival]
Queen Elizabeth II Diamond Jubilee Medal
